quick_wins: Delete 514 stale branches across 208 projects — zero risk, instant cleanup
Archive 62 test/demo projects to declutter the portfolio
Add justification for 44 projects with ≤5 unjustified false positives each — quick audit fix

ai_insights.narrative: The SonarCloud portfolio of 758 repositories is broadly functional but carries several material hygiene risks. The most significant are a large body of inactive projects (174 repositories with no analysis for over 2 years, 38 of them beyond 4 years), weak auditability around issue workflow (66 repositories marking false positives and 60 accepting issues without justification comments), and widespread branch sprawl (168 repositories with stale non-main branches). Configuration gaps in 50 repositories and 12 non-compliant Quality Gates further limit the consistency and trustworthiness of results, while 154 private repositories remain candidates for visibility review. Addressing the stale projects, restoring justification comments and tidying branch hygiene would materially improve the portfolio's governance posture.

sections.inactive_insights: 174 repositories have had no analysis for over 2 years, of which 38 have been inactive for more than 4 years (ea-address_lookup, ffc-elm-plan-service, ffc-elm-scheme-service). These stale projects inflate the portfolio view and retain LOC quota without delivering current value.
sections.inactive_action: Review ownership of the 174 inactive repositories and archive or delete the 38 dormant beyond 4 years before the next governance cycle.
sections.duplication_insights: 10 repositories exceed the duplication threshold, including ffc-digital-form-builder, phi-frontend and Defra.Trade.Notify.Ui, increasing maintenance effort and the risk of inconsistent fixes.
sections.duplication_action: Refactor the 10 high-duplication repositories to extract shared code and bring duplication back below the agreed threshold.
sections.migration_insights: 154 repositories are private (Defra.Trade.AIL.UI, Identity.App.Registration, Defra.Trade.Cloning.Processor), making them candidates for review under the SonarCloud visibility and migration policy.
sections.migration_action: Confirm the intended visibility of the 154 private repositories and progress any that should be migrated or made public in line with policy.
sections.branch_repos_non_standard_main_branch_in: 72 repositories are analysed on a branch other than main or master (Customer.CRM.All, Defra.Trade.AIL.UI, Defra.Trade.API.CertificatesStore), reducing the comparability of Quality Gate results across the portfolio.
sections.branch_repos_non_standard_main_branch_ac: Realign these 72 repositories so that main or master is the analysed branch.
sections.branch_repos_with_inactive_non_main_bran: 168 repositories carry non-main branches that have been inactive for 90 or more days (Customer.App.WriteAPI, Customer.CRM.All, adp-ffc-demo-calculation-service), cluttering analysis history and obscuring active work.
Remove or merge the stale non-main branches across the 168 affected repositories.
sections.branch_repos_with_non_main_branch_count_: 20 repositories exceed the non-main branch count threshold (apha-fps-apps, apha-vir, Defra.PTS.Web.UI), indicating accumulating branch sprawl.
Prune redundant branches in the 20 repositories above the threshold to restore a manageable branch count.
sections.naming_insights: 62 repositories use non-standard or unclear names (adp-ffc-demo-calculation-service, adp-ffc-demo-claim-service, adp-ffc-demo-payment-service), making ownership and purpose harder to determine.
sections.naming_action: Review the 62 oddly named repositories and rename or document them to clarify ownership and intended purpose.
sections.false_positives_insights: 102 repositories contain false-positive markings, and 66 of these (Wildlife Licensing Digital Service, fcp-audit-publisher, fcp-mpdp-backend) lack a justification comment, weakening the audit trail.
sections.false_positives_action: Require justification comments on the 66 repositories marking false positives without explanation and review them periodically.
sections.suppressions_insights: 97 repositories have accepted (suppressed) issues, with 60 of them (water-abstraction-system, ls-keeper-data-api, Identity.App.AccountManagement) recorded without a comment, reducing transparency over deliberately accepted risk.
sections.suppressions_action: Add justification comments to the 60 repositories with uncommented accepted issues and schedule periodic review.
sections.quality_gates_insights: 12 repositories are on non-compliant Quality Gates (Defra-APHA-CITES, defra-sps-apim-gateway, Identity.B2C.Policies), meaning their results are measured against a weakened or custom standard.
sections.quality_gates_action: Move the 12 repositories onto the compliant default Quality Gate and resolve any findings that surface as a result.
sections.config_gaps_insights: 50 repositories have missing or incomplete Sonar configuration (adp-ffc-demo-payment-service-core, adp-flux-notification-api, ai-tech-radar-backend), limiting the reliability of their scan results.
sections.config_gaps_action: Complete the scanner configuration for the 50 affected repositories to ensure consistent, comparable metrics.
sections.sonarcloud_rules_insights: In the defra organisation, the built-in 'Sonar way | PL/SQL' profile used by 14 projects has not been updated for 378 days, and the standalone '[OLD] DEFRA Javascript Default' profile does not inherit from a built-in profile; the defragovuk organisation has no profile findings.
sections.sonarcloud_rules_action: Update the long-stale PL/SQL built-in profile and either make the standalone JavaScript profile inherit from a built-in profile or document why it must remain separate.
